semanticCore: Staying, remaining, pausing, delay, wait, or slow reading
quranicRelevance: Staying/waiting for family (Makathtu - 'innee anastu naran... umkuthoo'), reading the Quran with pauses/intervals (Mukth - 'ala mukthin'), and staying forever.

keyDerivations.meaning: To stay, remain, abide, wait, pause, or tarry
Pause, delay, slow reading, or interval

figurativeOrRareUsages: لِتَقْرَأَهُ عَلَى النَّاسِ عَلَىٰ مُكْثٍ (17:106): That you may read it to the people at slow intervals/pauses (deep reflection style).
discardedNoise: Modern residency cards or visa stay permits.
Obscure details of static water pools (مكاث).
summaryEn: The root represents waiting and staying (Mukth), capturing Moses' instruction to his family to wait, and advising slow, paused recitation of the Quran.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر المكث والانتظار والتمهل، واصفاً أمر موسى لأهله بالانتظار (امكثوا)، وتوجيه ترتيل القرآن على تمهل وتؤدة (على مكث).
rawText: تَمَكَّثَ 5 see 1. سَارَ الرَّجُلُ مُتَمَكِّثًا The man journeyed, or proceeded, loitering; syn. مُتَلَوِّمًا. (S.) مَكَثَ 1 , aor. ﹹ; and مَكُثَ, aor. ﹹ; inf. n. مَكْثٌ [with which مُكْثٌ and مِكثٌ (see below) are syn.] and مِكِّيثَى (S, K) [like خِصِّيصَى, q. v.,] and مِكِّيثَآءُ [like خِصِّيصَآءُ] and مَكَثٌ and مُكُوثٌ and مُكْثَانٌ (K) and مَكَاثٌ and مَكَاثَةٌ [which last is the inf. n. of مَكُثَ]; (TA;) He tarried; tarried and waited, or expected; (S, K;) was patient, and tarried, and waited, or expected: or he tarried, stayed, or stopped, expecting: loitered; tarried; stayed; waited; paused in expectation; فِى مَكَانٍ in a place. (TA.) [In like manner,] 🡻تمكّث He loitered; tarried; stayed; waited; paused in expectation. (S, K.) مَكِيثٌ Grave: (S, K:) who does not hasten in his affair: pl. مُكَثَآءُ and مَكِيثُونَ. (TA.) === مَكِيثُ الكَلَامِ ‡ Slow of speech. (Ibn-Abi-l- Hadeed.) === Also مَكِيثٌ A man remaining; staying; abiding; remaining fixed, or stationary. (TA.) مُكْثٌ and 🡻مِكْثٌ, substs., from مَكَثَ or مَكُثَ, A tarrying; tarrying and waiting, or expecting; &c. (S, A.) مِكْثٌ : see مُكْثٌ.
